questions.question: What instrument did Olga play?
questions.explanation: The text says: 'She played the flute every day after school.'

questions.question: Olga made many mistakes at the concert.
questions.explanation: The text says: 'She didn't make a single mistake!'

questions.question: When did Olga practice?
questions.explanation: The text says: 'She practiced in the morning and in the afternoon.'

questions.question: How did Olga feel after the concert?
questions.explanation: The text says: 'Olga felt very proud.'

questions.question: A 'solo' means playing alone.
questions.explanation: A solo is a piece of music played by one person alone.

questions.question: Match the events in Olga's story in order.
Which is an adverb of time?
questions.explanation: 'Every day' tells us WHEN something happens — it is an adverb of time.

questions.question: Which is an adverb of time?
questions.explanation: 'Already' tells us WHEN — it is an adverb of time.

questions.question: Match each adverb of time with an example.

pairs.right: I wake up in the morning

rightOptions: I wake up in the morning

questions.question: Complete with the correct adverb of time.
Classify: adverb of time or not?
Complete with the correct word.
questions.sentence: She _____ knows the song. She memorized it _____.

questions.question: 'I practice _____ the morning.' Which preposition fits?
questions.explanation: We use 'in' with parts of the day: in the morning, in the afternoon, in the evening.

questions.question: Put the words in order to make a sentence.
Put the words in order to make a sentence.
What does 'have lunch' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Have lunch' means to eat the midday meal.

questions.question: Match each phrase with its meaning.
Olga gave up practicing because it was too hard.
questions.explanation: The text says: 'But Olga didn't give up.'

questions.question: Complete with 'in', 'at' or 'on'.
questions.sentence: I practice _____ the afternoon. The concert is _____ Saturday.

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'already' correctly?
questions.explanation: 'Already' goes before the main verb: 'She already plays the flute.'

questions.question: Classify these activities.
Why were Olga's hands sweaty?
questions.explanation: The text says her notes were wet because she was so nervous her hands were sweaty.

questions.question: Put the words in order to make a sentence.
The best musicians practice every day.
questions.explanation: The text says: 'The best musicians practice every day.'

questions.question: Complete with the correct words from Olga's story.
questions.sentence: Olga played a _____ at the concert. She didn't make a single _____!

questions.question: '_____ do you practice?' 'Every day after school.'
questions.explanation: 'When' asks about time: 'When do you practice?'

questions.question: Match each feeling with when Olga felt it.
